mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show positive mean returns when FEDFUNDS increases, or Rank IC remains below 0.02 over 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and impending economic contraction, which reduces corporate profit expectations and drives negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive in > 55% of instances when the unemployment rate crosses the threshold, or out-of-sample Sharpe ratio falls below 0.1.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent expansion in retail sales and housing starts confirms broad-based consumer demand strength, which directly boosts corporate revenue forecasts and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative forward returns in > 60% of triggered cases, or the interaction term loses statistical significance with p > 0.1 in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production indicates expanding manufacturing capacity and supply-side momentum, which supports near-term corporate earnings growth and yields positive S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: INDPRO growth fails to predict positive 5-day returns during early-cycle phases, or the signal reverses direction when inflation exceeds 3%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneously elevated consumer price inflation and restrictive policy rates create a stagflationary environment that compresses profit margins and equity multiples, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The combined high-inflation and high-rate regime yields positive forward returns in > 50% of observations, or the interaction effect is statistically indistinguishable from zero.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects a strengthening labor market and rising household income, which sustains consumer spending and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth shows no predictive power for 20-day returns, or Rank IC falls below 0.01 across all rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A quarterly decline in real GDP growth below the 1.0% threshold signals economic stagnation and reduced aggregate demand, which forces downward revisions to corporate earnings and generates negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns are positive in > 65% of quarters where GDP growth falls below the threshold, or the signal fails to outperform a buy-and-hold benchmark.

mechanism_narrative: Divergent momentum where industrial production rises while unemployment falls confirms robust economic expansion without labor market slack, supporting higher equity valuations and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative returns in > 55% of cases, or the predictive accuracy drops below random chance when tested out-of-sample.
